name: Executive Director or CEO Appointment Policy

extraction.text:
{CorporationName}
                                         (the "Corporation")

 Policy Name:                  {PolicyName}

 Policy Number:                {PolicyNumber}

 Effective Date:               {PolicyEffectiveDate}

 PREAMBLE

 The recruitment, selection and appointment of a CEO are, along with performance monitoring,
 among the most important responsibilities of the Board. An appointment of a CEO should be
 made with the confidence of the Board.

 POLICY:

 CEO Appointment:

 When a vacancy in the position of CEO is anticipated, the Board will determine the needs of the
 Corporation at that point in its evolution.

 The Board will establish an ad-hoc committee consisting of the Board Chair, Vice Chair, and at
 least one other member of the Board. The committee is tasked to:

  1. Establish timelines to determine successful appointment of the CEO.

  2. Conduct a community and philanthropic scan.

  3. Develop for the Board's review and approval a revised position description that addresses
     the needs and strategic direction of the Corporation.

  4. Present recommendations to the Board.

 The Board, with recommendations from the ad-hoc committee, will determine the search process.
 The Chair and Vice-Chair of the Board will implement the Board's direction.

 The selection of the final candidate shall be approved by the Board on a simple majority vote.

 The Chair and Vice-Chair of the Board are authorized to negotiate the employment (or contractor
 for an interim position) contract to secure term, duties/responsibilities and remuneration. The
 employment contract is presented to the Board for final approval.

 CEO Renewal:

  1. The Board will establish an ad-hoc committee of the Board Chair, Vice Chair, and at least
     one other member of the Board. The committee is tasked to:


Policy {PolicyNumber} - {PolicyName} - {CorporationName} - {PolicyEffectiveDate}
  2. Establish timelines to determine successful renewal of the CEO.

  3. Conduct a community and philanthropic scan for comparators.

  4. Review current position description that addresses the needs and strategic direction of the
     Corporation.

  5. Present recommendations to the Board.

 The Chair and Vice-Chair of the Board are authorized to negotiate the employment (or contractor
 for an interim position) contract to secure term, duties/responsibilities and remuneration. The
 employment contract is presented to the Board for final approval.

 The Committee shall report to the Board as and when required.

 Monitoring:                   This policy will be reviewed on or around {ReviewDate}.

 Board Acceptance:             This policy was approved by resolution of the board of directors
                               of the Corporation on {EffectiveDate}, as confirmed by
                               {#SoleVotDir}the signature of the sole director of the
                               Corporation below.{/SoleVotDir}{#MultiVotDir}the
                               counterpart signatures of each of the directors of the Corporation
                               below.{/MultiVotDir}

 {#VotingDirectors}


 {SignerTag}
 _________________________________________
 {Director-Name}{/VotingDirectors}


Policy {PolicyNumber} - {PolicyName} - {CorporationName} - {PolicyEffectiveDate}

                                         (the "Corporation")

 Policy Name:                  Executive Director or CEO Appointment Policy

 Policy Number:                HR-001

 Effective Date:               2026-01-01

 PREAMBLE

 The recruitment, selection and appointment of a CEO are, along with performance monitoring,

 The selection of the final candidate shall be approved by the Board on a simple majority vote.

 The Chair and Vice-Chair of the Board are authorized to negotiate the employment (or contractor
 for an interim position) contract to secure term, duties/responsibilities and remuneration. The
 employment contract is presented to the Board for final approval.

 CEO Renewal:

  1. The Board will establish an ad-hoc committee of the Board Chair, Vice Chair, and at least
     one other member of the Board. The committee is tasked to:


Policy HR-001 - Executive Director or CEO Appointment Policy - Sample Community Society - 2026-01-01
  2. Establish timelines to determine successful renewal of the CEO.

  3. Conduct a community and philanthropic scan for comparators.

  4. Review current position description that addresses the needs and strategic direction of the
     Corporation.

  5. Present recommendations to the Board.

 The Chair and Vice-Chair of the Board are authorized to negotiate the employment (or contractor
 for an interim position) contract to secure term, duties/responsibilities and remuneration. The
 employment contract is presented to the Board for final approval.

 Monitoring:                   This policy will be reviewed on or around 2027-01-01.

 Board Acceptance:             This policy was approved by resolution of the board of directors
                               of the Corporation on 2026-01-01, as confirmed by
                               the
                               counterpart signatures of each of the directors of the Corporation
                               below.

 


 Chair

sections.heading: Vacancy Planning
paragraphs: When an executive director or CEO vacancy is expected, the board determines organizational needs, timeline, search process, and interim authority.

sections.heading: Search Committee
bullets: The board appoints a committee with a chair, vice chair, and at least one additional board member or other approved participant.
The committee prepares timelines, comparator research, a position description, candidate process, and recommendations.
Final appointment requires board approval under the voting threshold set by the board or bylaws.

sections.heading: Contract
paragraphs: Only board-authorized people may negotiate employment or contractor terms, and the final agreement is reported to or approved by the board as required.

structuredSummaryTemplate.html:
{PolicyName}

Organization: {CorporationName}

Policy number: {PolicyNumber}

Effective date: {PolicyEffectiveDate}

This is a Societyer starter template remade from imported source material. Review it for your jurisdiction, bylaws, funder requirements, charity status, and actual operations before adoption.

Vacancy Planning

When an executive director or CEO vacancy is expected, the board determines organizational needs, timeline, search process, and interim authority.

Search Committee

  • The board appoints a committee with a chair, vice chair, and at least one additional board member or other approved participant.
  • The committee prepares timelines, comparator research, a position description, candidate process, and recommendations.
  • Final appointment requires board approval under the voting threshold set by the board or bylaws.

Contract

Only board-authorized people may negotiate employment or contractor terms, and the final agreement is reported to or approved by the board as required.

Monitoring

This policy will be reviewed on or around {ReviewDate} and whenever legal, operational, funding, or governance requirements materially change.

Board Acceptance

This policy was approved by resolution of the board of directors of {CorporationName} on {EffectiveDate}.

{#SoleVotDir}Approved by the sole voting director.{/SoleVotDir}{#MultiVotDir}Approved by counterpart signatures of the voting directors.{/MultiVotDir}

{#VotingDirectors}{SignerTag}
_________________________________________
{Director-Name}{/VotingDirectors}

Source: ExecutiveDirectorCEOAppointment.pdf; SHA-256 bd40655e834fe2ea22aac84cdc3b53b5dc0be3545452e3e777cd5b3cd082eed6
